epics.title: Foundation & Infrastructure
epics.description: Next.js scaffold, Supabase project, schema migrations, RLS policies, Mapbox integration, master club seed. Dependency for every other epic.

epics.title: Tournament Setup (Admin)
epics.description: Four-step wizard to create tournament, configure course holes, drop pin coordinates on satellite map, and activate. Makes every other admin and player feature workable.

epics.title: Registration & Profile
epics.description: Invite-driven player self-registration (4-step), team search, captain/concierge mode for bulk team registration, profile page. Enables players to enter the system.

epics.title: Pre-Round Setup
epics.description: Post-login tournament home with countdown to tee, team confirmation, bag review, and Start Round CTA. Bridges registration to active play.

epics.title: Round Tracking
epics.description: The core experience. GPS shot capture, Mapbox map view, club selection, outcome handling (In Play / OOB / Mulligan / Sunk), OOB rehit linkage, edit prior shot, foursome turn picker, hole summary. The riskiest epic by far.

epics.title: Scoring Engine
epics.description: PostgreSQL RPC functions for Best Ball calculation, provisional vs final hole status, team standings view. Variable team_size support. Phase 1 ships Best Ball only.

epics.title: Leaderboard
epics.description: Public shareable leaderboard at /t/[slug]/leaderboard (no auth, SSR, sponsor branded) and post-login team leaderboard with sticky current-team card. Realtime via Supabase Realtime channel with client coalescing and 30s polling fallback.

epics.title: Admin Operations
epics.description: Admin panel for live ops dashboard, player management, score editing with audit trail, team assignment, master club list management. Tournament organizer role scopes admin actions per tournament.

epics.title: Offline & Sync
epics.description: IndexedDB shot queue with write-through pattern, sync-on-reconnect with newer-wins conflict resolution, offline UI indicator. Phase 1 ships IndexedDB only; Service Worker proper deferred to Phase 2.

epics.title: Security & 2FA
epics.description: SMS 2FA enrollment and challenge, password reset, admin force-2FA, audit log for admin actions, score dispute flow. Deferred to Phase 2 — Phase 1 ships with email + password only.

stories.title: As a developer, I want a Next.js 14 App Router project scaffolded with TypeScript, Tailwind, shadcn/ui, and Supabase client, so that all subsequent epics can build on a consistent foundation.

acs.text: Next.js 14 App Router project boots locally with `npm run dev` and renders a placeholder page
TypeScript strict mode enabled; `tsconfig.json` extends Next.js defaults
Tailwind CSS configured with PostCSS pipeline; sample utility class renders
shadcn/ui CLI initialized; `components/ui` directory exists with at least Button installed
`@supabase/ssr` and `@supabase/supabase-js` installed; browser + server client factories in `lib/supabase/`
`.env.local.example` documents `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY`, `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_MAPBOX_TOKEN`

stories.title: As a developer, I want a Supabase project provisioned with PostgreSQL and email auth enabled, so that we have a backend ready for schema migrations and authentication.

acs.text: Supabase project created (cloud or local via `supabase start`)
Email + password auth provider enabled in Supabase Auth settings
Project URL and anon key documented in `.env.local`
`supabase/config.toml` checked into repo for reproducible local dev

stories.title: As a player, I want the FDgolf — built with AI/RUN app chrome on every authenticated page, so that the brand identity is consistent across the entire app.

acs.text: `AppChrome` component renders dark forest-green (#0e2818) header bar
Header displays "FDgolf" with green "FD" mark (#6ee7a0) and white "golf"
Header displays "built with AI/RUN" tagline with AI/RUN logo on the right
AppChrome is responsive (mobile, tablet, desktop)
Used in the root `app/layout.tsx` so all routes inherit it

stories.title: As a player, I want to log in with email and password and stay logged in across page navigation, so that I don't have to re-authenticate constantly during my round.

acs.text: `/login` page renders email + password form with FDgolf branding
Successful login redirects to intended route (default `/`)
Failed login displays an error message without exposing whether the email exists
Session persists via cookies set by Supabase SSR client
`/logout` route clears the session and redirects to `/login`
Next.js middleware refreshes the session on every request

stories.title: As a developer, I want the complete DB schema migrated, so that all subsequent feature work has the tables it needs.

acs.text: Migration creates tables: tournaments, courses, holes, players, user_roles, tournament_registrations, teams, rounds, shots, shot_edits, shot_attestations, hole_scores, team_hole_scores, clubs, tournament_clubs, score_disputes

All enums defined: tournament_format, tournament_start_style, tournament_status, role_type, registration_status, round_status, shot_outcome, rehit_origin_type, hole_score_status, club_type, dispute_status
Foreign keys and unique constraints match the data model in design spec section 4
`teams.team_size` int column with check constraint (2 <= team_size <= 5)
`shots.updated_at` defaults to now() and triggers update on row change
Migration runs cleanly on a fresh database via `supabase db reset`

stories.title: As a developer, I want Row Level Security policies enforced on every table, so that tournament data is isolated and roles are respected.

acs.text: RLS enabled on every table created in US-0005
Public read on tournaments, team_hole_scores, clubs (drives the public leaderboard and registration landing)
Players can read self + own team members; admin bypass exists
Players can write shots only during own round
tournament_organizer scoped to their tournament_id
Public payload on team_hole_scores includes player name + company only (no email, phone, year_of_birth, gender)
SQL tests verify each policy with a non-privileged JWT

stories.title: As a player, I want a Mapbox map ready to render in the app, so that round tracking can show me my position and the hole pin.

acs.text: `mapbox-gl` and `react-map-gl` installed
`MapView` component renders a Mapbox map centred on a passed-in lat/lng
Custom golf-course-style URL configurable via env var with sensible default
Component renders without errors when used in a Client Component
Mapbox token loaded from `NEXT_PUBLIC_MAPBOX_TOKEN` env var; missing token shows a friendly fallback message instead of crashing

stories.title: As an admin, I want the master club list seeded with a standard golf bag, so that tournaments can use it out of the box.

acs.text: Seed migration inserts 15 standard clubs: Driver, 3-wood, 5-wood, 3-hybrid, 4–9 iron, PW, GW, SW, LW, Putter
Each club has `club_type`, `default_loft_degrees` (except Putter), `display_order`, `is_active=true`
Display order matches a real golfer's bag traversal (Driver → wedges → Putter)
Seed is idempotent (re-running does not create duplicates)

stories.title: As an admin, I want to create a tournament with basic info, so that registration and play can be configured around it.

acs.text: Form fields: name (req), starts_at date+time (req), venue (req), format (req, defaults Best Ball), start_style (req, defaults shotgun), holes_count (req, defaults 18)
On submit, tournament is created with status=draft
Slug auto-generated from name (lowercase, hyphens, ascii-only)

stories.title: As an admin, I want to override the auto-generated slug, so that the public URL matches the printed invitations.

acs.text: Slug field is editable on the basics step of the wizard
Uniqueness check runs on blur; conflict shows inline error
Only lowercase letters, digits, hyphens accepted

stories.title: As an admin, I want to enter par, yardage, and stroke index for each hole, so that the scoring engine and round map have the data they need.

acs.text: Course holes table renders one editable row per hole (1 to holes_count)
Par values constrained to 3, 4, 5
Stroke indices constrained to 1–18, unique within the course
Total par computed and displayed at the bottom
Save persists rows to the `holes` table linked to the tournament's course

stories.title: As an admin, I want a Granite Ridge GC preset to one-tap import, so that I don't hand-key 18 rows for the first tournament.

acs.text: "Import preset" button shows a list of available presets (at least Granite Ridge GC)
Importing populates the 18-hole table with par, yardage, stroke index
Admin can then edit any value before saving

stories.title: As an admin, I want to drop pin coordinates for each hole on a satellite map, so that the round tracking app can compute distance-to-pin.

acs.text: Satellite map renders for each hole at a sensible default zoom around the course
Click on the map drops a pin; coordinates saved to holes.pin_lat / pin_lng
Tee coordinates can also be dropped (separate mode)
Progress bar shows N of 18 holes with pins set
"Save and continue to next hole" button auto-advances
Holes table shows pin status (Set or Missing) per hole

stories.title: As a player, I want a Mapbox Static API PNG snapshot stored per hole, so that distance-to-pin works even if the Mapbox tile API is rate-limited mid-round.

acs.text: On pin save, a request is made to Mapbox Static API for the hole region
PNG is uploaded to Supabase Storage in `course-maps/{courseId}/hole-{n}.png`
`holes.static_map_url` populated with the public URL

stories.title: As an admin, I want to pick which clubs from the master list are available in this tournament, so that I can enforce sponsor-supplied bags or simplified club lists.

acs.text: All master clubs listed with toggle controls; defaults to all-active
Disabled clubs are excluded from the player's bag picker in pre-round setup

stories.title: As an admin, I want CIBC tournament sponsor logos (First Derivative, AI/Run) hardcoded for the first tournament, so that the public leaderboard ships on schedule.

acs.text: Logos shipped as static assets in `public/sponsors/`
Tournament with slug `cibc-arc-2024` (or current first tournament's slug) shows both logos on public leaderboard
Tournaments with other slugs show no sponsor logos until admin upload UI ships in Phase 2

stories.title: As an admin, I want a pre-flight checklist before activation, so that I don't open registration on a misconfigured tournament.

acs.text: Checklist shows green checks for: basics complete, all holes configured (par 72 or expected), all 18 pin coordinates set, slug verified unique
Checklist shows amber reminders for: clubs configured, teams unassigned
Activate button disabled until all green checks pass
Live preview card shows what the public leaderboard banner will look like

stories.title: As an admin, I want buttons to move the tournament between statuses, so that I can open registration, start play, and close the tournament cleanly.

acs.text: From draft, "Open registration" moves status to registration_open
From registration_open, "Start tournament" moves status to active
From active, "Complete tournament" moves status to completed
Paused status reachable via DB direct update; no UI in Phase 1
Transitions are logged with timestamp and admin actor

stories.title: As an admin, I want the public registration URL displayed after activation, so that I can copy-paste it into the invite emails.

acs.text: Post-activation confirmation screen shows the URL `fdgolf.app/register/{slug}`
Copy-to-clipboard button

stories.title: As an admin, I want to promote a player to tournament_organizer for a specific tournament, so that ops responsibility can be delegated without granting global admin.

acs.text: Admin can search any player and click "Make organizer" for a tournament
user_roles row inserted with role=tournament_organizer and tournament_id=this_tournament
Organizer can perform admin actions only on rows where tournament_id matches their role's tournament_id

stories.title: As a player, I want a tournament-branded invite landing page, so that I know what I'm signing up for before I start the form.

acs.text: Route `/register/{slug}` resolves the tournament from the slug
Tournament name, venue, date, format displayed
Sponsor logos visible (if configured)
"Register" CTA and "I have an account" link

stories.title: As a player, I want to enter my personal info, so that the system knows who I am and how to contact me.

acs.text: Required fields: full name, title, company, email, mobile phone
Optional fields explicitly labelled: year of birth (opt), gender (opt with prefer-not-to-say)
Email format and phone format validated client-side and server-side
Form values persist across step navigation

stories.title: As a player, I want to set a password with confirmation, so that I can log in next time.

acs.text: Two password fields with match validation
Minimum 8 characters required in Phase 1 (Phase 2 strengthens)
On submit, account created via Supabase Auth and player row inserted
Player is auto-logged-in after account creation

stories.title: As a player, I want to choose my team and search for foursome members, so that I'm grouped with my colleagues for play.

acs.text: Team number selector lists 1 to tournament max teams
Member search returns existing registered players by name or email
"Add member" can add placeholder rows for non-registered players (email-only)
tournament_registrations row created with team_id linked

stories.title: As a team captain, I want to register and assign all my team members in one flow, so that non-tech executives don't have to use the app themselves.

acs.text: During registration, player can check "I'm the team captain"
Captain dashboard at /team/{teamId}/captain accessible post-login
Captain can add new team members by name + email
Each added member gets a signed invite link sent (stub email; logged to console in Phase 1)
Invite link goes to a one-step "Set password" page with profile pre-filled by captain

stories.title: As a developer, I want a signed invite link generator and verifier, so that captain-issued invites are tamper-proof.

acs.text: Generator produces a JWT signed with Supabase service role key containing player_id and team_id
Verifier rejects expired (>14 days) and invalid signatures
Email send is stubbed in Phase 1 (logged); real send wired in Phase 2

stories.title: As a player, I want a profile page where I can view and edit my info, so that I can correct mistakes and see my tournament history.

acs.text: Route `/profile` shows current player's profile
Editable: name, title, company, phone, year of birth, gender
Read-only: email, handicap (Phase 2 edits handicap)
Tournament history list (Phase 1 shows current tournament only)

stories.title: As a player, I want to request a password reset link, so that I can recover my account if I forget my password.

acs.text: `/forgot-password` accepts email, calls Supabase Auth reset
Confirmation shown ("Check your inbox") regardless of whether the email exists
Reset link from email opens `/reset-password?token=...` page where new password can be set

stories.title: As an admin, I want teams to support 2-, 3-, 4-, or 5-player sizes, so that drop-outs and oversized teams are handled cleanly.

acs.text: Team form has team_size selector 2-5 (default 4)
Team detail UI says "Foursome" when team_size=4, "team of N" otherwise
Best Ball calculation in EPIC-0006 uses actual team_size (not hardcoded 4)

stories.title: As a player, I want a tournament home page after I log in, so that I know my tee time, my team, and where to start.

acs.text: Countdown to starts_at displayed at top
Team card with all members and their company
Starting hole pin card with par, yardage
"Start Round" primary CTA
"View leaderboard" secondary link

stories.title: As a player, I want to confirm or remove clubs from my bag before I start, so that the picker shows only clubs I actually have.

acs.text: Tournament club list rendered as toggleable chips
Defaults all on
Tap to remove (greys out)
Bag selection persisted to the round record

stories.title: As a player, I want to confirm who goes first, so that the team starts in the right order.

acs.text: "Going on first" defaults to current player
"Change" button opens picker with team members

stories.title: As a player, I want my round record created when I tap Start Round, so that subsequent shots have a parent.

acs.text: Inserts rounds row with status=in_progress, start_hole=tournament-assigned, started_at=now()
Redirects to /round/{roundId} after creation

stories.title: As a player, I want a Begin Hole X entry screen, so that I see the map, distance, and club picker before I take my first shot.

acs.text: Map view shows hole pin, my GPS location, tee marker
Distance-to-pin displayed (approx prefix)
Club picker shows tournament bag with smart default (Driver on tee, otherwise last-used)
"Start shot — capture GPS" CTA

stories.title: As a player, I want the active hole map view to show my shot trail, so that I can see where I've been and where the pin is.

acs.text: Map shows fairway base layer from cached Mapbox Static URL
Hole pin marker rendered
Tee marker rendered
Prior shots rendered as dashed lines + numbered markers
Current player position pulsed in red
Distance-to-pin overlay top-left

stories.title: As a player, I want to capture my GPS position when I take a shot, so that the system knows where I hit from.

acs.text: navigator.geolocation.getCurrentPosition called with high accuracy
lat/lng captured to local state on Start Shot tap
Selected club persisted with the shot draft

stories.title: As a player, I want four outcome buttons after I hit a shot, so that I can record what happened.

acs.text: Four buttons: In Play (neutral green), Sunk (filled green), Mulligan (amber), OOB (red)
In Play / Sunk / Mulligan tap inserts shots row with appropriate stroke_count (1 / 1 / 0)
OOB tap triggers the OOB rehit prompt (US-0038)

stories.title: As a player, I want to choose where my rehit comes from after OOB, so that the recorded GPS origin is accurate.

acs.text: Prompt offers "Rehit from OOB location" or "Rehit from prior position"
OOB shot inserted with stroke_count=2 (1 for shot + 1 penalty)
Follow-up shot's origin_lat/lng set to chosen rehit position
rehit_from_shot_id and rehit_origin set on the follow-up shot

stories.title: As a player, I want Mulligan to not count as a stroke, so that I can reshoot without penalty.

acs.text: Mulligan outcome inserts shot with stroke_count=0
Next shot's origin defaults to same location

stories.title: As a player, I want Shot Sunk to close the hole for me, so that the hole score is recorded and the turn moves on.

acs.text: Sunk outcome inserts shot with stroke_count=1
Computes gross_score as sum of stroke_count on this hole
Inserts/updates hole_scores row with status=provisional or final
Triggers team_hole_scores recalc via EPIC-0006

stories.title: As a player, I want to edit any prior shot in this round, so that I can correct mistakes without admin help.

acs.text: Tap a shot in the map or hole summary to open the edit panel
Editable: club, outcome, GPS origin
Before/after states recorded in shot_edits audit table
shots.updated_at and updated_by set on save
Hole scores recalculated if outcome or stroke_count changed

stories.title: As a player, I want the foursome turn picker to auto-select the next player by farthest from pin, so that the team plays in golf order without thinking.

acs.text: After a shot is recorded, compute distance-to-pin for each team member's last unfinalized shot
Auto-select the player with greatest distance
Manual override available
Players with sunk outcome on current hole excluded
Works with team_size 2-5

stories.title: As a player, I want a hole summary after all teammates hole out, so that I can see who scored what and our team total.

acs.text: Per-player gross score listed with par-relative annotation (e.g., "bogey", "+2")
"BEST" badge on the contributing player whose score won the hole
Team standing pre-loaded (current position out of N)
"Next: Hole X" CTA where X is the next physical hole (handles shotgun start wrap)

stories.title: As a player, I want to move to the next hole, so that play continues smoothly.

acs.text: Next advances to next physical hole accounting for 18-hole wrap from shotgun start
New hole map renders; shot stream resets

stories.title: As a player, I want a "Hole X of 18" pill, so that I can see tournament progress regardless of which physical hole I'm on.

acs.text: Pill shows holes completed by team (1-18) plus current hole

stories.title: As a player, I want my round automatically marked complete when all 18 holes are scored, so that I'm not stuck in an "active" state.

acs.text: When all 18 hole_scores are final, rounds.status set to completed and completed_at set
Player is shown a "Round complete" screen with final score

stories.title: As a player, I want manual coordinate entry if GPS permission is denied, so that I can still record shots.

acs.text: If geolocation permission denied, show explanation and "Tap the map" instructions
Click on the map sets the shot's origin coordinates manually

stories.title: As a player, I want distances shown as approximate, so that I'm not misled by GPS accuracy limits.

acs.text: Display format "~245 yds to pin" with the approx prefix
GPS accuracy (in metres) stored on the shot record (optional column)

stories.title: As a developer, I want a PostgreSQL function calc_best_ball_for_hole, so that team scoring is computed server-side consistently.

acs.text: Function signature: calc_best_ball_for_hole(p_team_id uuid, p_hole_number int) returns (best_score int, contributing_player_id uuid, status hole_score_status)
Returns min gross_score across team members for the hole
Status is final only when all team_size members have final hole_score for this hole
SQL tests verify with 2-, 3-, 4-, 5-player teams

stories.title: As a developer, I want a DB trigger on hole_scores that upserts team_hole_scores, so that team totals stay in sync without app-side bookkeeping.

acs.text: AFTER INSERT/UPDATE on hole_scores calls calc_best_ball_for_hole
team_hole_scores row created or updated with returned values
status (provisional/final) flows from the function result

stories.title: As a developer, I want score-vs-par helpers, so that the leaderboard can show "-2 to par" without app-side math.

acs.text: SQL view returns per-hole and cumulative score-vs-par
Handles missing holes (returns null cumulative until first hole completes)

stories.title: As a developer, I want a team_standings SQL view, so that the leaderboard query is a single fetch.

acs.text: View aggregates team_hole_scores into total score, thru count, score-vs-par
Sorted by score ascending, then thru descending
Includes rank column with proper tied handling

stories.title: As a developer, I want provisional vs final status to be deterministic, so that the leaderboard never disagrees with the round.

acs.text: hole_scores.status=final when player has outcome=sunk OR shot count on hole > 8 (auto-finalize blowout)
team_hole_scores.status=final only when all team_size members have final hole_score for the hole

stories.title: As a developer, I want test coverage of Best Ball edge cases, so that scoring bugs don't blow up the tournament.

acs.text: Tests cover variable team_size (2, 3, 4, 5)
Mulligan shots (stroke_count=0) do not inflate scores
OOB penalty stroke is correctly included in gross_score
Withdrawn players excluded from team Best Ball calc

stories.title: As a player, I want provisional team scores shown distinctly on the leaderboard, so that I know which holes aren't fully scored yet.

acs.text: team_hole_scores.status returned in leaderboard payload
Client renders italic grey for provisional, solid for final

stories.title: As a spectator, I want a public leaderboard URL at /t/{slug}/leaderboard, so that I can follow the tournament without logging in.

acs.text: SSR; renders fast first-paint
Tournament header: name, venue, date, sponsor logos
No auth required
Basic Open Graph meta tags (og:title, og:description)
Privacy guard: only name + company shown for players

stories.title: As a player, I want my team's position prominent on the post-login leaderboard, so that I can see how we're doing at a glance.

acs.text: Hero card with green gradient pinned above the top-20 list
Shows team #, members, current rank, score, thru count
Shown regardless of where my team ranks

stories.title: As a player, I want a LIVE indicator on the leaderboard, so that I trust the data is fresh.

acs.text: Red blinking pill when websocket connected
"AUTO 30s" pill when polling-only

stories.title: As a player, I want the leaderboard to update in real time, so that I see new scores as they come in.

acs.text: Client subscribes to Supabase Realtime channel tournament:{slug}
Broadcasts on team_hole_scores changes only
Reconnects on disconnect

stories.title: As a player, I want leaderboard updates coalesced, so that the UI doesn't thrash during heavy scoring activity.

acs.text: Multiple incoming events within 5s coalesce into one render
Uses requestAnimationFrame for batching

stories.title: As a player, I want polling fallback when websocket fails, so that I'm not stuck with stale data.

acs.text: If websocket fails or stays disconnected >10s, switch to 30s polling
Resume websocket when it comes back available

stories.title: As a player, I want to drill into a team to see their hole-by-hole scores, so that I can see how they're playing.

acs.text: Tap team row opens detail view
9-hole strip x 2 (front and back nine)
Rows: par, best score per hole
Birdies+ highlighted gold
Provisional scores italic grey

stories.title: As a player, I want personal data hidden on the public view, so that my privacy is respected.

acs.text: Public leaderboard payload omits email, phone, year_of_birth, gender
Server-side filter enforced (not client-side)

stories.title: As a spectator, I want a clear indication when the tournament is paused, so that I understand the standings might not be live.

acs.text: When tournaments.status=paused, banner shows "Tournament paused"
LIVE pill disabled; data still visible

stories.title: As an admin, I want a persistent left sidebar layout, so that I can navigate quickly between sections.

acs.text: Sidebar with 8 items: Dashboard, Tournaments, Players, Teams, Scores, Courses, Clubs, Stats
Items grouped (operational vs setup)
Current section highlighted
Route-protected to admin role

stories.title: As an admin, I want 4 KPI stat cards on the dashboard, so that I can monitor tournament health at a glance.

acs.text: Players card shows registered count and recent delta
Teams playing card shows in_progress rounds count
Avg pace card shows computed minutes-per-hole vs target
Sync issues card shows count and team numbers
Auto-refresh every 30s

stories.title: As an admin, I want a live rounds table with pace warnings, so that I can chase slow groups before they back up the course.

acs.text: Table shows all in_progress rounds with team, players, thru, score, pace
Rows where pace exceeds target by more than 2 min/hole highlighted amber
Pace target configurable per tournament (default 12 min/hole)
Click row to view the team's round

stories.title: As an admin, I want to search and filter players, so that I can find specific people fast.

acs.text: Server-side search by name, email, or company
Chip filters: Unassigned, No 2FA, by company
Pagination at 50 per page
Row actions: Edit, Reset PW, Delete

stories.title: As an admin, I want to edit player profiles, so that I can fix mistakes and reset passwords.

acs.text: Edit modal with all profile fields
Reset PW sends Supabase Auth recovery email
Force-2FA toggle (Phase 2 enforcement)

stories.title: As an admin, I want to delete a player with confirmation, so that I can clean up test accounts.

acs.text: Soft delete; preserves shot history for audit
Player marked withdrawn in registration
Confirmation dialog required

stories.title: As an admin, I want to drill into a round and edit any shot, so that I can resolve disputes.

acs.text: Round detail page shows holes 1-18 with shot lists
Each shot row is editable: club, outcome, GPS, distance
Save persists shot_edits audit row with admin actor
Hole scores and team_hole_scores recompute via trigger

stories.title: As an admin, I want the audit trail visible per shot, so that I can defend score changes.

acs.text: Shot edit panel shows all changes with timestamp and author
Admin edits shown amber for visual distinction

stories.title: As an admin, I want to assign and reassign players to teams, so that late changes can be made.

acs.text: Player row has team selector (dropdown or drag-drop)
team_size respected on assignment
Unassigned pool shown separately

stories.title: As an admin, I want to manage the master club list with drag-to-reorder, so that I can keep it tidy.

acs.text: Drag handles on each row
Toggle active per club
Edit display name, loft
Soft delete on remove

stories.title: As an admin, I want sync issues flagged automatically, so that I can intervene without spotting them manually.

acs.text: Round flagged sync_issue when no shot insert AND no GPS heartbeat for >10 min during active round AND current hole not marked complete
Background job (or cron RPC) updates a sync_issue field on rounds
Dashboard KPI card shows count; click to drill in

stories.title: As an admin, I want a list of all tournaments, so that I can switch context between events.

acs.text: List shows all tournaments with name, date, status pill
Filter by status
Click enters tournament context (sets active tournament in admin session)

stories.title: As a developer, I want IndexedDB set up via idb library, so that the shot queue has a reliable client storage layer.

acs.text: idb library installed
Versioned schema with shots store (key=tempId)
Database opens on app boot

stories.title: As a player, I want shots written to IndexedDB first, so that no data is lost when the network drops.

acs.text: Every shot insert writes IndexedDB first with status=pending
Background process drains pending shots to Supabase
On Supabase success, IndexedDB row status=synced

stories.title: As a player, I want a visible offline indicator with queue depth, so that I know when sync is pending.

acs.text: Banner appears when navigator.onLine=false
Banner shows queue depth (e.g., "3 shots queued")
Auto-dismisses when online and queue is empty

stories.title: As a player, I want my queue to drain on reconnect with newer-wins resolution, so that my latest edits stick.

acs.text: Online event triggers queue drain
Upsert by id; server compares updated_at and rejects older
Rejected upserts logged to dev console for debugging

stories.title: As an admin, I want my edits to always win over client edits, so that disputes don't get overwritten by stale offline queues.

acs.text: Admin edit sets updated_by=admin_id and updated_at=now()
Server-side trigger rejects any subsequent client upsert with older updated_at

stories.title: As a tester, I want an offline manual test pass, so that I'm confident the queue actually works on iOS Safari and Android Chrome.

acs.text: Test matrix documented: iOS 16/17/18 x Safari/Chrome x foreground/background mid-shot x airplane-mode toggle
Each cell run manually with pass/fail captured
Failures opened as BUGs in BUGS.md

stories.title: As a player, I want to enroll in 2FA via SMS, so that my account is more secure.

acs.text: Profile page has "Enable 2FA" CTA
SMS sent to registered phone with verification code
Code verification enrolls the factor in Supabase Auth

stories.title: As a player, I want a 2FA challenge on login, so that password compromise alone isn't enough to access my account.

acs.text: When factor exists, login prompts for SMS code after password
"Remember this device" option for 30 days

stories.title: As an admin, I want to force 2FA per player, so that high-privilege accounts must enroll.

acs.text: Admin toggle on player edit screen
Next login forces enrollment before accessing the app

stories.title: As an admin, I want a log of sensitive admin actions, so that I can audit changes after the fact.

acs.text: admin_audit_log table created
Records every admin action with target, before/after, timestamp
Admin can view audit log filtered by date or actor

stories.title: As a player, I want to flag a hole score for dispute, so that I can request a review without admin help.

acs.text: Round summary has a "Dispute" button per hole
Dispute creates a score_disputes row with reason text
tournament_organizer sees disputes queue
Resolve or dismiss closes the dispute with a note

stories.title: As a developer, I want stronger password rules, so that accounts are harder to compromise.

acs.text: Min 12 chars, mixed case, number, symbol
Strength meter on password fields
Server-side validation matches client rules

stories.title: As a player, I want real emails for invites and password reset, so that I can use the app outside dev mode.

acs.text: SMTP integration (Resend or AWS SES) configured
Branded HTML templates for invite, password reset, 2FA codes
Email send logs captured for support
